ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) Exchange w3wp.exe → ASPX web shell drop & LOLBin recon chain (ProxyLogon post-ex)
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon kill chain on on-prem Exchange: post-auth ProxyLogon-class exploitation results in IIS worker (w3wp.exe) writing .aspx/.ashx web shells (China Chopper, ChinaChopper variants, OWA-style shells) into Exchange virtual directories AND/OR w3wp.exe spawning native LOLBin recon (cmd, net, whoami, systeminfo, ipconfig). Both halves were the signature MSTIC/Volexity IOCs from Operation Exchange Marauder.
ucs.splunk: (| tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx") (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\V15\\FrontEnd\\*") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | eval evidence="web_shell_drop") | append [| tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval evidence="w3wp_lolbin_child"] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort 0 - lastTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let ExchangePaths = dynamic([@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\", @"\ClientAccess\", @"\V15\FrontEnd\", @"\Exchange Server\"]);
let WebShellDrops = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
    | where FolderPath has_any (ExchangePaths)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              Evidence = "WebShellDrop",
              FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
              InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFolderPath;
let W3wpChildren = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              Evidence = "W3wpLolbinChild",
              FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, SHA256 = "";
union WebShellDrops, W3wpChildren
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 30d;
let ExchangePathRegex = @"(?i)\\(inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client|FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\(owa|ecp)\\auth|ClientAccess|V15\\FrontEnd|Exchange Server)\\";
let WebShellDrops = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActingProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
    | where TargetFileExtension in~ ("aspx","ashx","asmx")
    | where TargetFilePath matches regex ExchangePathRegex
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              Evidence = "WebShellDrop",
              TargetFileName, TargetFilePath, TargetFileSHA256,
              ActingProcessCommandLine;
let W3wpChildren = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith "w3wp.exe"
    | extend Child = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where Child in ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","quser.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","ping.exe","netstat.exe","reg.exe","nltest.exe","hostname.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe")
    | project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname = Computer, ActorUsername = Account,
              Evidence = "W3wpLolbinChild",
              TargetFileName = Child, TargetFilePath = NewProcessName, TargetFileSHA256 = "",
              ActingProcessCommandLine = CommandLine;
union WebShellDrops, W3wpChildren
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: MSTIC's HAFNIUM (Silk Typhoon) reporting on the four Exchange 0-days (CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065) and Volexity's Operation Exchange Marauder writeup both pinned the post-exploitation pattern to (a) the IIS worker w3wp.exe writing China Chopper-style .aspx/.ashx shells into specific Exchange virtual paths (\aspnet_client\, \HttpProxy\owa\auth\, \HttpProxy\ecp\auth\) and (b) w3wp.exe immediate

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) LSASS credential dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump or Procdump (post-ProxyLogon)
ucs.description: Hunts the OS-credential-dump step that MSTIC explicitly attributed to HAFNIUM after web-shell access: rundll32.exe loading C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll with the MiniDump export pointed at lsass.exe PID, OR Sysinternals procdump targeting lsass. Pairs with the Exchange UC above to surface the full initial-access → cred-theft chain.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where ( (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*" (Processes.process="*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*#24*" OR Processes.process="* 24 *")) OR (Processes.process_name IN ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") (Processes.process="*lsass*" OR Processes.process="*-ma*")) OR (Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") (Processes.process="*MiniDumpWriteDump*" OR Processes.process="*Out-Minidump*" OR Processes.process="*Get-Process lsass*")) ) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT (user="*$" OR match(user,"(?i)^(SYSTEM|LOCAL SERVICE|NETWORK SERVICE)$")) | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort 0 - lastTime
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
| where
    (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
        and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll"
        and (ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump" or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\bcomsvcs\.dll[, ]+#?24\b"))
    or (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
        and (ProcessCommandLine has "lsass" or ProcessCommandLine has "-ma"))
    or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
        and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDumpWriteDump","Out-Minidump","PssCaptureSnapshot")
        and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
          IsFromWebShell = iif(InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe" or InitiatingProcessParentFileName =~ "w3wp.exe", "YES_HAFNIUM_CHAIN", ""),
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where AccountType == "User"
| extend ImageLower = tolower(NewProcessName), CmdLower = tolower(CommandLine)
| where
    (ImageLower endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
        and CmdLower has "comsvcs.dll"
        and (CmdLower has "minidump" or CmdLower matches regex @"comsvcs\.dll[, ]+#?24\b"))
    or (ImageLower endswith "\\procdump.exe" or ImageLower endswith "\\procdump64.exe")
        and (CmdLower has "lsass" or CmdLower has "-ma")
    or ((ImageLower endswith "\\powershell.exe" or ImageLower endswith "\\pwsh.exe")
        and (CmdLower has "minidumpwritedump" or CmdLower has "out-minidump" or CmdLower has "psscapturesnapshot")
        and CmdLower has "lsass")
| extend IsFromWebShell = iif(tolower(ParentProcessName) endswith "\\w3wp.exe", "YES_HAFNIUM_CHAIN", "")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, IsFromWebShell, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) LSASS Credential Dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump or Procdump
id: 7c2b5f9a-1d4e-4a83-9f2b-silktyphoon0125
status: experimental
description: Detects HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon (G0125) post-exploitation LSASS memory dumping via rundll32 loading comsvcs.dll's MiniDump export (#24) or procdump targeting lsass.exe. Documented by MSTIC and Volexity as the credential-access stage following ProxyLogon Exchange web-shell access.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_comsvcs_minidump:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'comsvcs.dll'
      - 'MiniDump'
  selection_comsvcs_ordinal:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'comsvcs.dll'
    CommandLine|re: 'comsvcs\.dll[, ]+#?24\b'
  selection_procdump:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'lsass'
  selection_ps_minidump:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MiniDumpWriteDump'
      - 'Out-Minidump'
      - 'PssCaptureSnapshot'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'lsass'
  filter_machine_account:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: 1 of selection_* and not filter_machine_account
falsepositives:
  - Authorised red-team / IR memory-capture engagements
  - Some legitimate crash-dump tooling targeting non-LSASS processes (filter on lsass keyword reduces this)
level: high
ucs.rationale: MSTIC's original HAFNIUM blog (Mar 2021) and the follow-up DART writeup both called out comsvcs.dll MiniDump and Procdump targeting lsass as the credential-access step Silk Typhoon ran from the planted web shells — a tradecraft choice that's still in their playbook because it lives off built-in signed binaries and avoids dropping mimikatz. The rule pivots on the comsvcs.dll + MiniDump (or ordinal
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
